feedback.text: Hello! I am writing to you as a concerned citizen from Germany to bring to your attention an issue that I believe falls under the scope of the Digital Services Act (DSA). It has come to my attention that a video game I purchased from the French company Ubisoft, titled "The Crew" was taken away from me without prior notice or a valid reason. This incident highlights a broader problem regarding digital ownership and consumer rights in the gaming industry. Ubisoft first shut down the servers for "The Crew" which were not even necessary for the game to function, effectively making it inaccessible. They then silently revoked my access to the game entirely. As a consumer who purchased this game with my own money, I find it unjust that companies can take away digital purchases without any warning or explanation. This issue is not isolated; currently, there is a petition with over 1 million signatures aimed at preventing similar incidents in the future. The difference between physical and digital ownership is huge. I can still access all my games on discs, cartridges, and even floppy discs. However, with digita only games, I risk losing access permanently if the company shuts down its service or bans my account. Ubisoft has even stated that consumers should get comfortable with not owning their games. If this trend continues, consumers in the EU could lose thousands to millions of game copies, along with their potential historic or artistic value. Addressing this issue is crucial for protecting consumer rights and maintaining trust in the digital marketplace. I urge the EU to take action to ensure that consumers are not deprived of their lawful purchases without just cause. Thank you for your time.
